meta.setting: French Gulch mining settlement at the foot of dry hills and worked creek ground
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in mining-town grit and public tension

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Mining settlement, road, logging, and ranch pressure around Whiskeytown.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Gold-rush town architecture and Northern California commerce.

image_prompt.subject: a rough French Gulch main street with ore wagons, false-front stores, plank walk, miners, riders, and a stamp mill visible down the grade
image_prompt.scene: late afternoon dust and wood smoke; two men argue beside an ore wagon, a rider waters a horse, children watch from a porch, no readable signage
image_prompt.composition: square settlement establishing plate with main street cutting diagonally through the frame and creek works visible beyond town
image_prompt.camera: slightly low third-person street view, like a player entering town from the wagon road
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make the town feel useful, sour, and watched from every porch.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine town vista
render_traits: slightly simplified settlers, riders, miners, and team animals
modest texture resolution on timber, dust, cloth, iron, and hillside scrub
hard ambient occlusion under porches, wagons, horses, and awnings
warm late-day light with dirty street haze
public tension staged like an explorable mission hub without copying any known game location
style_modifiers.palette: dust gold, gray weathered timber, creek mud brown, faded red paint, dark iron, smoke blue, and dry-grass ochre.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable store signs, posters, crate labels, or text.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos
empty town with no human pressure

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
French Gulch, ore wagons, plank walk, creek works, riders, and miners are readable.
Every object belongs plausibly to an 1899 Northern California mining settlement.
The image supports table scenes about work, debt, public argument, and road travel.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
